name: Stanley Clarke
description: American bassist, film composer and founding member of Return to Forever
abstract: Stanley Clarke (born June 30, 1951) is an American bassist, composer and founding member of Return to Forever, one of the first jazz fusion bands. Clarke gave the bass guitar a prominence it lacked in jazz-related music.

concerts.label: Newport Jazz Festival: Stan Getz Quartet, Modern Jazz Quartet, and Pharoah Sanders Quintet

about_quotes.speaker: Chick Corea
about_quotes.evidence: That was the first version of Return to Forever... Stanley and I just... We have this agreement that has lasted through the decades of stuff... We love to play together.
about_quotes.doc_title: Chick Corea — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Lonnie Liston Smith
about_quotes.evidence: we had Stanley Clarke on bass and Airto was playing drums... So we were in Philadelphia... they were going to California to form their group Return to Forever
about_quotes.doc_title: Lonnie Liston Smith interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, January 9, 2021

about_quotes.speaker: Alvin Queen
about_quotes.evidence: I spent five years with the Horace Silver Quintet... Stanley Clarke... I’m trying to think of all the people who were in Horace’s band when I was in there.
about_quotes.doc_title: Alvin Queen interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, August 23, 1997 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Kenny Barron
about_quotes.evidence: I worked with him once, the first time... I took Chick Corea’s place... It was Tony Williams and Stanley Clarke... I loved working with him.
about_quotes.doc_title: Kenny Barron — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Bela Fleck
about_quotes.evidence: Like I’ve got this trio I’m supposed to do. It’s Stanley Clarke and Jean Luc Ponty... we’ve never played together
about_quotes.doc_title: Bela Fleck interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, December 7, 2004

about_quotes.speaker: Wilbur Ware
about_quotes.evidence: he's using Stanley and myself, two basses. I'm listening to Stanley and Stanley say, "You want me to play this?"
about_quotes.doc_title: 68088_Wilbur B. Ware, Sr oral history interview
